net.openhft.chronicle.core.util

Class Ints

  • java.lang.Object
    • net.openhft.chronicle.core.util.Ints

  • public final class Ints
extends Object

      static void assertIfEnabled(IntBiPredicate requirement, int value, int otherValue)
      Checks that the provided value and provided otherValue satisfies the provided requirement.

      Checks that the provided value, provided otherFirstValue and provided otherSecondValue satisfies the provided requirement.

      Returns a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value did not satisfy the provided requirement.

      Returns a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value, provided otherFirstValue and provided otherFirstValue did not satisfy the provided requirement.

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, toString, wait, wait, wait

    • Method Detail

      • require

        public static int require(IntPredicate requirement,
                          int value)
                   throws IllegalArgumentException,
                          NullPointerException
        Checks that the provided value satisfies the provided requirement.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in public methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.require(IntCondition.NON_NEGATIVE, bar);
 }
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided value
        value - the value to check
        Returns:
        the provided value if the check passes
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails
        NullPointerException

      • require

        public static int require(IntBiPredicate requirement,
                          int value,
                          int otherValue)
                   throws IllegalArgumentException,
                          NullPointerException
        Checks that the provided value and provided otherValue satisfies the provided requirement.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in public methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.require(IntBiCondition.GREATER_THAN, bar, 42);
 }
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided value and otherValue
        value - the value to check
        otherValue - the other value to compare against the provided value
        Returns:
        the provided value if the check passes
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails
        NullPointerException

      • require

        public static int require(IntTriPredicate requirement,
                          int value,
                          int otherFirstValue,
                          int otherSecondValue)
                   throws IllegalArgumentException,
                          NullPointerException
        Checks that the provided value, provided otherFirstValue and provided otherSecondValue satisfies the provided requirement.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in public methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.require(IntTriCondition.BETWEEN, bar, 13, 42);
 }
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided values
        value - the value to check
        otherFirstValue - the other first value to compare against the provided value
        otherSecondValue - the other first value to compare against the provided value
        Returns:
        the provided value if the check passes
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • assertIfEnabled

        public static void assertIfEnabled(IntPredicate requirement,
                                   int value)
                            throws AssertionError,
                                   NullPointerException
        Asserts that the provided value satisfies the provided requirement.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in public methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.require(IntCondition.NON_NEGATIVE, bar);
 }
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided value
        value - the value to check
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.
        AssertionError - if the check fails and assertions are enabled both via the -ea JVM command line option and by setting AssertUtil.SKIP_ASSERTIONS to false.

      • assertIfEnabled

        public static void assertIfEnabled(IntBiPredicate requirement,
                                   int value,
                                   int otherValue)
                            throws AssertionError,
                                   NullPointerException
        Checks that the provided value and provided otherValue satisfies the provided requirement.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in public methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.require(IntBiCondition.GREATER_THAN, bar, 42);
 }
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided value and otherValue
        value - the value to check
        otherValue - the other value to compare against the provided value
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.
        AssertionError - if the check fails and assertions are enabled both via the -ea JVM command line option and by setting AssertUtil.SKIP_ASSERTIONS to false.

      • assertIfEnabled

        public static void assertIfEnabled(IntTriPredicate requirement,
                                   int value,
                                   int otherFirstValue,
                                   int otherSecondValue)
                            throws AssertionError,
                                   NullPointerException
        Checks that the provided value, provided otherFirstValue and provided otherSecondValue satisfies the provided requirement.

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in public methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.require(IntTriCondition.BETWEEN, bar, 13, 42);
 }
        Parameters:
        requirement - to impose on the provided values
        value - the value to check
        otherFirstValue - the other first value to compare against the provided value
        otherSecondValue - the other first value to compare against the provided value
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.
        AssertionError - if the check fails and assertions are enabled both via the -ea JVM command line option and by setting AssertUtil.SKIP_ASSERTIONS to false.

      • failDescription

        public static String failDescription(IntPredicate requirement,
                                     int value)
                              throws NullPointerException
        Returns a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value did not satisfy the provided requirement.
        Parameters:
        requirement - to imposed on the provided values
        value - the value to check
        Returns:
        a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value did not satisfy the provided requirement
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.

      • failDescription

        public static String failDescription(IntBiPredicate requirement,
                                     int value,
                                     int otherValue)
                              throws NullPointerException
        Returns a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value and provided otherValue did not satisfy the provided requirement.
        Parameters:
        requirement - to imposed on the provided values
        value - the value to check
        otherValue - the other value to compare against the provided value
        Returns:
        a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value and provided otherValue did not satisfy the provided requirement
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.

      • failDescription

        public static String failDescription(IntTriPredicate requirement,
                                     int value,
                                     int otherFirstValue,
                                     int otherSecondValue)
                              throws NullPointerException
        Returns a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value, provided otherFirstValue and provided otherFirstValue did not satisfy the provided requirement.
        Parameters:
        requirement - to imposed on the provided values
        value - the value to check
        Returns:
        a human readable form of a failure message provided that the provided value and provided otherValue did not satisfy the provided requirement
        Throws:
        NullPointerException - if the provided requirement is null.

      • requirePositive

        public static int requirePositive(int val)
                           throws IllegalArgumentException
        Checks that the provided val is positive (i.e. val > 0)

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.requirePositive(bar);
 }
        Parameters:
        val - the value to check
        Returns:
        the provided val if the check passes
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • requireNegative

        public static int requireNegative(int val)
                           throws IllegalArgumentException
        Checks that the provided val is negative (i.e. val < 0)

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.requireNegative(bar);
 }
        Parameters:
        val - the value to check
        Returns:
        the provided val if the check passes
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • requireZero

        public static int requireZero(int val)
                       throws IllegalArgumentException
        Checks that the provided val is zero (i.e. val == 0)

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.requireZero(bar);
 }
        Parameters:
        val - the value to check
        Returns:
        the provided val if the check passes
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • requireNonPositive

        public static int requireNonPositive(int val)
                              throws IllegalArgumentException
        Checks that the provided val is non-positive (i.e. val <= 0).

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.requireNonPositive(bar);
 }
        Parameters:
        val - the value to check
        Returns:
        the provided val if the check passes
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • requireNonNegative

        public static int requireNonNegative(int val)
                              throws IllegalArgumentException
        Checks that the provided val is non-negative (i.e. val >= 0).

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.requireNonNegative(bar);
 }
        Parameters:
        val - the value to check
        Returns:
        the provided val if the check passes
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • requireNonZero

        public static int requireNonZero(int val)
                          throws IllegalArgumentException
        Checks that the provided val is non-zero (i.e. val != 0).

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.requireNonZero(bar);
 }
        Parameters:
        val - the value to check
        Returns:
        the provided val if the check passes
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • requireEquals

        public static int requireEquals(int val,
                                int otherVal)
                         throws IllegalArgumentException
        Checks that the provided val equals the provided otherVal (i.e. val == otherVal).

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.requireEquals(bar, 42);
 }
        Parameters:
        val - the value to check
        Returns:
        the provided val if the check passes
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • requireNotEquals

        public static int requireNotEquals(int val,
                                   int otherVal)
                            throws IllegalArgumentException
        Checks that the provided val does not equal the provided otherVal (i.e. val != otherVal).

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.requireNotEquals(bar, 13);
 }
        Parameters:
        val - the value to check
        Returns:
        the provided val if the check passes
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • requireInRange

        public static int requireInRange(int val,
                                 int from,
                                 int toExclusive)
                          throws IllegalArgumentException
        Checks that the provided val is between the provided from (inclusive) and the provided toExclusive (exclusive) (i.e. val ∈ [from, toExclusive)).

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.requireInRange(bar, 8, 16);
 }
        Parameters:
        val - the value to check
        Returns:
        the provided val if the check passes
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails

      • requireInRangeClosed

        public static int requireInRangeClosed(int val,
                                       int from,
                                       int toInclusive)
                                throws IllegalArgumentException
        Checks that the provided val is between the provided from (inclusive) and the provided toInclusive (inclusive) (i.e. val ∈ [from, lastExclusive]).

        This method is designed primarily for doing parameter validation in methods and constructors, as demonstrated below: 

         public Foo(int bar) {
     this.bar = Ints.requireInRangeClosed(bar, 8, 15);
 }
        Parameters:
        val - the value to check
        Returns:
        the provided val if the check passes
        Throws:
        IllegalArgumentException - if the check fails
